MINI MINUTES FOR JUN-06-2018 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS' MOTION TO APPROVE PLAINTIFFS' INDIVIDUAL PAGA SETTLEMENT IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO REFILING THE MOTION BASED ON A FULLER AND MORE PERSUASIVE RECORD. NO PROOF OF SERVICE OF THE MOVING PAPERS ON THE LWDA. THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT IS NOT INCLUDED IN THE MOVING PAPERS. THERE IS NO PERSUASIVE EXPLANATION WHY PLAINTIFFS SOUGHT TO REPRESENT OTHER ALLEGEDLY AGGRIEVED EMPLOYEES AND THEN AGREED TO A SETTLEMENT PROVIDING THEM NO RELIEF. MOST SIGNIFICANTLY, THERE IS NO QUALITATIVE OR QUANTITATIVE ANALYSIS FOR THE PLAINTIFFS' PAGA CLAIMS NOR IS THERE ANY PERSUASIVE EXPLANATION WHY SO LITTLE OF THE SETTLEMENT AMOUNT HAS BEEN ALLOCATED TO PAGA PENALTIES. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR # 8600. =302/HK

Off Calendar Dec-10-2018 Off Calendar

Plaintiff Melody Esquivel, Margaret Clee, Abninder Bains' Renewed Motion To Approve Plaintiffs Individual Paga Settlement
Off Calendar Dec-05-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Plaintiff Melody Esquivel, Margaret Clee, Abninder Bains' Renewed Motion To Approve Plaintiffs Individual Paga Settlement.

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiffs' motion to approve the PAGA settlement is granted. Plaintiffs have shown that the settlement, including the fees sought by their counsel, falls within the broad scope of reasonableness for a PAGA settlement.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-04-2018 Continued To Dec-11-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Plaintiffs Individual Paga Settlement
Off Calendar Jun-06-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Alex G. Tovarian of e Employment Law & Personal Injury, Tel. No. 1-415-984-9990
appearng for the Plaintiff;
Atty. Robert I. Lockwood of Jackson Lewis, Tel. No. 1-415-796-5467 appearing
for the Defendant.

RULING - The matter was discussed. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiffs' motion to approve plaintiffs' individual PAGA settlement is denied without prejudice to refiling the motion based on a fuller and more persuasive record. No proof of service of the moving papers on the LWDA. The settlement agreement is not included in the moving papers. There is no persuasive explanation why plaintiffs sought to represent other allegedly aggrieved employees and then agreed to a settlement providing them no relief. Most significantly, there is no qualitative or quantitative analysis for the plaintiffs' PAGA claims nor is there any persuasive explanation why so little of the settlement amount has been allocated to PAGA penalties. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR # 8600. =302/hk
